description: This is an automatically generated playbook based on the question: A detections playbook for T1548 of MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise framework is required.The target platform is Windows. You can find more information at https://attack.mitre.org/detectionstrategies/DET0345#AN0975 . The description of the approach follows: Correlate registry modifications (e.g., UAC bypass registry keys), unusual parent-child process relationships (e.g., control.exe spawning cmd.exe), and unsigned elevated process executions with non-standard tokens or elevation flags.

external_references.name: MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1548: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism

items.content: Deploy and configure a SIEM or log aggregation tool to collect and correlate events from Windows endpoints.
Enable and configure Windows Event Logging to capture the necessary events: Security, System, and Microsoft-Windows-Windows Defender/Operational logs.
Implement a detection rule to monitor for registry modifications related to UAC bypass techniques.
Monitor process creation events (Event ID 4688 or Sysmon Event ID 1) for known suspicious parent-child pairs (e.g., control.exe spawning cmd.exe).
Establish a baseline of normal parent-child process relationships for the environment.
Alert on deviations from the baseline or known malicious patterns.
Create a detection rule for unusual parent-child process relationships
Monitor for process creation events where the integrity level or token elevation type is unusual (e.g., high integrity from a non-administrative parent).
Check for processes running with elevated privileges (e.g., via Token Elevation Type) that are unsigned or from untrusted locations.
Correlate with events indicating UAC bypass or privilege escalation attempts.
Detect unsigned elevated process executions with non-standard tokens or elevation flags
Correlate alerts from registry modifications, unusual process relationships, and elevated process executions to identify potential T1548 abuse.
Investigate correlated alerts to determine if they indicate malicious activity or false positives.
If malicious activity is confirmed, initiate incident response procedures to contain and eradicate the threat.
